vulnerabilities.description: Fallback to anonymous Supabase key in server action that creates/modifies data. If SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is missing, code uses NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY which has limited permissions via RLS.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY env var not set → anonymous key used → sensitive operations may fail or have wrong permissions → data integrity issues
vulnerabilities.impact: Data integrity violation, unexpected behavior in critical server actions

vulnerabilities.description: Same credential fallback issue - uses anonymous key if service role key missing when creating invoices (financial transaction).
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Missing SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY → invoice creation uses limited anonymous key permissions → potential data loss or write failures on financial records

vulnerabilities.description: CRON_SECRET uses simple string equality comparison instead of constant-time comparison. Vulnerable to timing attacks that could reveal the secret byte-by-byte.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Attacker makes many requests with gradually increasing CRON_SECRET guesses → timing differences between early vs late rejections → secret recoverable via statistical analysis

vulnerabilities.description: Same timing attack vulnerability as blog route - CRON_SECRET comparison lacks constant-time protection.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Attacker exploits timing differences to recover CRON_SECRET → unauthorized access to leads follow-up automation
vulnerabilities.impact: Authentication bypass for critical automation endpoints

vulnerabilities.description: CRON_SECRET comparison uses direct string equality, vulnerable to timing attacks across all cron endpoints.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Attacker performs timing analysis on multiple cron endpoints → derives CRON_SECRET → full control over automated tasks
vulnerabilities.impact: Complete compromise of all cron-based automations

vulnerabilities.description: Invoice endpoint only validates presence of razon_social and cif, but doesn't validate their format. CIF should have specific format validation.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: User sends invoice with invalid CIF format → accepted and stored → downstream processing fails
vulnerabilities.impact: Data quality issues in financial processing

vulnerabilities.description: CSV export in exportMaterialsCSV() doesn't sanitize formula injection characters (=, +, -, @). Excel/Sheets will interpret as formula.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Admin creates material named '=1+1' → exported CSV → Excel interprets as formula → potential code execution
vulnerabilities.impact: CSV formula injection allowing arbitrary command execution in spreadsheet applications

vulnerabilities.description: Same CSV formula injection in exportProjectsCSV() - client names not sanitized.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Project with client name starting with '=cmd|' → exported CSV → formula injection when opened in Excel
vulnerabilities.impact: CSV formula injection in project export

vulnerabilities.description: Multiple console.error() calls log raw error objects which may contain sensitive database information and stack traces.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Database error → console.error logs full error object → stack trace and database URLs visible in server logs → information disclosure

vulnerabilities.description: Invoice creation endpoint lacks rate limiting. Financial transactions should be protected against abuse.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Attacker sends 1000 invoice creation requests → spam invoices created → database DoS / financial record corruption
vulnerabilities.impact: Denial of service, financial record tampering

vulnerabilities.description: Blog generation endpoint lacks rate limiting. Could be exploited for resource exhaustion (OpenAI API calls are expensive).
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: Attacker derives cron token via timing attack → calls blog endpoint repeatedly → massive OpenAI charges
vulnerabilities.impact: Resource exhaustion, financial damage from API overuse

vulnerabilities.description: Basic Authentication transmits credentials in base64 (not encrypted). Should use bearer tokens instead.
vulnerabilities.failure_scenario: If HTTPS is downgraded or proxy intercepts → credentials exposed in base64 encoding
vulnerabilities.impact: Weaker authentication method compared to bearer tokens/API keys

summary: High-severity security issues found related to authentication and credential management. Most critical: (1) timing attacks on CRON_SECRET due to missing constant-time comparison, (2) unsafe fallback to anonymous Supabase keys in server actions, and (3) CSV formula injection in export functions. Medium-severity issues include missing input validation, lack of rate limiting, and error logging exposure.
recommendations: CRITICAL: Fix timing attacks on CRON_SECRET by replacing string comparison with crypto.timingSafeEqual across all cron endpoints
CRITICAL: Remove fallback to anonymous Supabase key - throw error if SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is missing in server actions
HIGH: Implement CSV formula injection prevention by prefixing values starting with =, +, -, @ with single quote
HIGH: Add rate limiting on all API endpoints using @vercel/speakeasy or similar
MEDIUM: Fix error logging to not expose raw error objects or database information
MEDIUM: Add CIF format validation and email format validation in invoice creation
MEDIUM: Verify CSRF tokens are configured for all state-changing endpoints
LOW: Consider migrating from HTTP Basic Auth to bearer token authentication
LOW: Add startup validation for all required environment variables before accepting requests
